name: WebGPU API
performance_characteristics: Modern explicit GPU API exposing rendering and compute shaders; lower driver overhead and better multi-threading than WebGL, at high implementation complexity
unsuitable_contexts: older browsers and devices without WebGPU support
strengths: Modern explicit GPU API with first-class compute shaders, not just rendering
Lower CPU/driver overhead and better parallelism than WebGL
Enables in-browser GPU compute for ML, simulation and large-scale visualization
WGSL shading language and a cleaner resource/pipeline model
weaknesses: Newer baseline; support arrived later and remains limited on older browsers and many mobile/older devices, so a WebGL fallback is usually required
Highest complexity of the graphics APIs; verbose pipeline and bind-group setup
Heavy GPU and battery usage; unsuitable for low-power or dense enterprise UI
No DOM semantics or built-in accessibility; ecosystem still maturing
